differences: HOME initial mobile viewports now closely match the prototype, with intentional darker text/mint values and 44px ingredient chips for accessibility gates.
Sort-open state remains visually different because the prototype has three sort options while MVP keeps the official four-option API contract.
The 320px sort menu opens upward to keep all official options visible on a short viewport, while the prototype reference shows a shorter menu opening downward.
suggestions: Keep the HOME shell and card spacing fixed as the current Phase 4 mobile baseline.
Do not add prototype-only 빠른 조리순 without contract-evolution approval.
In the next HOME polish pass, tune dropdown width and row height without changing official sort values.
next_actions: Ship the HOME mobile Phase 4 slice as the first small PR.
Continue Phase 4 with the next mobile reference group after this baseline is merged.
reasoning: The first HOME mobile pass is strong enough to become the current implementation baseline, but exact parity is limited by official sort options and accessibility contrast/touch-target gates.
